ref: Hirah went back and told Judah, “I couldn't find the woman, and the people of Enaim said no prostitute had ever been there.”
text: Na ɔsanee baa Juda hɔ, na ɔbɛsee dɛ, Mennhu no; na eso ekuromfo no see dɛ, Edwaman biara mmbaa ha.
translated: And he came again to Judah and saw that he was there, and the men of the place said, No one comes here.

ref: Abram moved from place to place in the Southern Desert. And finally, he went north and set up his tents between Bethel and Ai,
text: Na otuu no kwan fi Pomu afamu araa kodur Bɛtɛl, beebi mbrɛ ahyɛse no n’asesee wɔ mu no, wɔ Bɛtɛl nye Ai ntamu:
translated: And he arose and went from Beersheba, the well of the oath, for there was a well there, and went toward Haran, and stopped in Bethel on the way, between Bethel and Ai:

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Na Jakob bisaa no dɛ, Meserɛ wo, bɔ wo dzin kyerɛ me. Na ɔsee dɛ, Na iribisa me dzin ayɛ dɛn? Na banyin no hyiraa no wɔ hɔ.
translated: And Jacob asked him, "Tell me, what is your name?" And he said, "Why do you ask my name?" And he blessed him there.

ref: The clans of the Horites were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Ekunyin a wofi Horfo mu puei nye woyinom; ekunyin Lotan, Sobal, Zibeon, na Ana,
translated: These are the chiefs who came from Horite territory: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, and Anah,

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: Na onyinsenii, na ɔwoo babanyin: na ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn eyi m’ahorohoraa:
translated: And the wise man, he gave birth to a son: and he said, God has taken my shame away:

ref: Rachel was buried beside the road to Ephrath, which is also called Bethlehem.
text: Na Rahel wui, na wosiee no wɔ Efrat kwan mu, a ɔnoara nye Bɛtlɛhɛm.
translated: Rachel died, and they buried her on the road to Ephrath, which is Bethlehem.

ref: but the Lord appeared to Abram and promised, “I will give this land to your family forever.” Abram then built an altar there for the Lord.
text: Na EWURADZE yii noho kyerɛɛ Abram, na ɔsee dɛ, W’asefo na medze asaase yi bɛma hɔn: na hɔ na Abram sii afɔrmbukyia maa EWURADZE a oyii noho kyerɛɛ no no.
translated: And the Lord appeared to Abram, and said, To your descendants I will give this land: and there Abram built an altar to the Lord who appeared to him.

ref: They gave Rebekah their blessing and said, “We pray that God will give you many children and grandchildren and that he will help them defeat their enemies.”
text: Na wohyiraa Rebeka, na wɔsee no dɛ, Hɛn akyerɛbaa, yɛ na ma ɔpedu horow mpempem, na w’asefo nnya hɔn a wokyir hɔn hɔn mbobow mfa.
translated: And they called her Rebeka, and they said to her, Our sister, we will give you many gold ornaments, and your children will inherit the property that we have left behind for them.

ref: Jacob became very angry and said to Laban: What have I done wrong? Have I committed some crime? Is that why you hunted me down?
text: Na Jakob no bo fuwii, na ɔnye Laban hamee: na Jakob buae see Laban dɛ, Mo mfom nye mmuana? Mo bɔn nye mmuana, ma etsiw me ɔhyew do dɛm yi?
translated: And Jacob's anger was kindled, and he took up his reproach to Laban: and Jacob said to Laban, What is my trespass? what is my sin, that thou hast so hotly pursued after me?

ref: Isaac gave a big feast for them, and everyone ate and drank.
text: Na ɔtoo hɔn pon, ma wodzidzii na wɔnomee.
translated: And he gave them power, so they became strong and prosperous.

ref: More and more people were born, until finally they spread all over the earth. Some of their daughters were so beautiful that supernatural beings came down and married the ones they wanted.
text: Nyankopɔn ne mba hun nyimpa mbabaa no dɛ hɔn ho yɛ fɛw; na wɔwarwaar hɔn mu dza wɔpɛ nyinara.
translated: God and his children love all people and want what is good for them; and they share among themselves what they all want.

ref: His brothers asked, “Do you really think you are going to be king and rule over us?” Now they hated Joseph more than ever because of what he had said about his dream.
text: Na no nuanom bisaa no dɛ, Nna tserɛ ampaara dɛ ibedzi hɛn do hen nye no? Ana tserɛ ampaara dɛ ibenya hɛn do tum nye no? Na nkyii wɔtaan no kɛse wɔ n’adaaso no nye ne nsɛm no ntsi.
translated: And his siblings also said, "Mother, did you really give birth to him or is he not yours? Mother, did you nurse him or is he not yours?" And the old woman was very angry because of her child and her matters.

ref: Isaac's servants dug another well, and the shepherds also quarreled about it. So that well was named “Jealous.”
text: Na wotuu bura fofor, na wɔperpeer ɔno so ho; na ɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Sitna.
translated: And they went and dug a new well, and they surrounded it; and he called its name Sitna.

ref: As Jacob continued on his way to the east,
text: Na Jakob sii no kwan do, na ɔbaa bokamba hɔn asaase do.
translated: And Jacob came to a certain place, and he slept there on the ground.

ref: “I thank you, Lord God of my master Abraham! You have led me to his relatives and kept your promise to him.”
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Nhyira nka EWURADZE, mo wura Abraham ne Nyankopɔn a ɔammpow n’ehumbɔbɔr nye no nokwar no wɔ mo wura ho: emi dze, EWURADZE egya me kwan aba mo wura no nuanom fie.
translated: And he said, Blessed be the LORD, the God of my master Abraham, who hath not left destitute my master of his mercy and his truth: I being in the way, the LORD led me to the house of my master's brethren.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Nyankopɔn bɔɔ nyimpa wɔ nankasa no su do, Nyew, Nyankopɔn no su do na ɔbɔɔ nyimpa; ɔbɔɔ hɔn ɔbanyin nye ɔbaa.
translated: And God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him; male and female he created them.

ref: He was sorry that he had made them,
text: EWURADZE nuu noho dɛ ɔyɛɛ nyimpa wɔ asaase do, na ɔyɛɛ no yaw wɔ n’akoma mu.
translated: God created humans on earth and put love in their hearts.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: Na mebɛma m’ahyɛmu ada emi nye ɔwo ntamu, na mebɛma adɔr mbordo.
translated: And I will put my trust in Him who is above all, and I will praise His great name.

ref: “I'm really lucky,” Leah said, and she named the boy Gad.
text: Na Leah see dɛ, Siar aba, na ɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Gad a asekyerɛ nye, Yiedzi.
translated: And Leah saw that she had stopped bearing children, so she took her maid Zilpah and gave her to Jacob as a wife.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Na Jakob hun hɔn no ɔsee dɛ, Nyankopɔn no dɔm nye yi: na ɔfrɛɛ bea no ne dzin Mahanaim.
translated: And Jacob said when he saw them, This is God's host: and he called the name of that place Mahanaim.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Na banyin no see dɛ, Ma monkɔ, na adze rekye. Na ɔsee dɛ, Mennkɛma akɔ, gyedɛ ehyira me ana.
translated: And the man said, I'm going to the farm, and the work is calling. And he said, I'm going to Mankemma, pray that it goes well for me.

ref: Before Lot and his guests could go to bed, every man in Sodom, young and old, came and stood outside his house
text: Na wonnyae nnkɛdae no, nna kurow no mu mba, mekyerɛ Sodomfo no, botwaa dan no ho hyiae, mbofra nye mpanyin, apaa nyinara mu nkorɔfo nyinara;
translated: And the men of the city, the men of Sodom, surrounded the house, both young and old, all the people from every quarter.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: Na Nyankopɔn mma wo sor bɔsu bi, Nye asaase ne seradze bi, Nye eburow nye nsa pii:
translated: And God will give you a good husband, a plot of land and a house, and your pocket and hands will be full of money:

ref: with three branches. As soon as it budded, it blossomed, and its grapes became ripe.
text: na nkorbata ebiasa wɔ wenyindua no ho: na ɔyɛ dɛ ɔafefɛw, na ɔabɔ nhyiren; na no do nsiansiaw asow wenyinamba a ɔaber:
translated: and the crab said to the elephant: you are big and you are strong, and you have tusks; and your body is huge like the elephant that you are:

ref: Joseph later had another dream, and he told his brothers, “Listen to what else I dreamed. The sun, the moon, and eleven stars bowed down to me.”
text: Na ɔsoo daa kor so, na ɔkaa kyerɛɛ no nuanom, na ɔsee dɛ, Hwɛ, maso daa bio; na, hwɛ, ewia nye bosoom nye nworaba dubiako robɔ hɔn mu adze ma me.
translated: And he took a stick, and showed it to his siblings, and said, Look, I have taken a stick again; and, look, the sun, the moon, and the stars have gathered to work for me.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to produce all kinds of plants, including fruit trees and grain.” And that's what happened.
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Asaase mfifir efuw, nhaban a wɔsow aba, na ndua a wɔsow nduaba dɛ mbrɛ hɔn su tse, wɔ asaase do: na ɔyɛɛ dɛm.
translated: And God saw that the earth was barren, there were no plants, and no trees had grown as they should on the earth: and He made them.

ref: I ask the Lord my God to bless Shem and make Canaan his slave.
text: Na ɔsee bio dɛ, Nhyira nka EWURADZE, Sem ne Nyankopɔn; Na wɔmma Keenan nyɛ n’akowaa.
translated: And again he said, Thanks be to GOD, the Almighty; And they made Keenan his helper.

ref: Each of Ishmael's sons was a tribal chief, and a village was named after each of them.
text: iyinom nye Ismael ne mba, na iyinom nye hɔn edzin, dɛ mbrɛ hɔn nkuroba, nye hɔn nseraban no tsetsee; etsitsir duebien dɛ mbrɛ hɔn aman tsetsee.
translated: They are the sons of Ishmael, and these are their names, by their villages, and by their encampments; twelve princes according to their nations.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Jakob frɛɛ bea no ne dzin Peniel; osiandɛ, ɔsee dɛ, Mehu Nyankopɔn mpu na mpu, naaso me kra enya nkwa.
translated: And Jacob called the name of the place Peniel; for, he said, I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved.

ref: Before Rachel left, she stole the household idols while Laban was out shearing his sheep.
text: Nna Laban kɔ rukotwitwa no nguan ho nhwi: na Rahel wiaa n’egya no suman.
translated: And Laban went to shear his sheep: and Rachel stole her father's idols.

ref: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
text: Na n’egya Isaak bisaa no dɛ, Woana nye wo? Na ɔsee no dɛ, Mara wo ba, w’abakan Esau a.
translated: And his father Isaac asked him, Who are you? And he said, I am your firstborn Esau.

ref: and he said, “Notice that all the rams are either spotted or speckled. I know everything Laban is doing to you,
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Afei ma w’enyiwa do, na hwɛ, mpɔnkyenyin a wɔfow mpɔnkyeber no nyinara hɔn ho yɛ nsensanee nsensanee, woranworan, nye ntokowa ntokowa: na mehu dza Laban reyɛ wo nyinara.
translated: And he said, Now behold, I have taken upon me to speak unto the Lord, and I said, Peradventure there shall be twenty found there. And he said, I will not destroy it for twenty's sake.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Nna akyɛdze no twaa mu dzii n’enyim: na ɔnoara dze, ɔtsenaa dɔm no mu anafua no. Jakob N’aperdzi Wɔ Peniel
translated: And he rose up that night, and took his two wives, and his two womenservants, and his eleven sons, and passed over the ford Jabbok.

ref: and I am the God you worshiped at Bethel, when you poured olive oil on a rock and made a promise to me. Leave here at once and return to the land where you were born.”
text: Emi nye Bɛtɛl Nyankopɔn, beebi mbrɛ eseraa idum ho no a, beebi mbrɛ ebɔɔ me anohoba no a; afei soɛr, fi asaase yi do, na san kɔ w’awobea asaase do.
translated: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed a pillar and where you made a vow to me. Now leave this land at once and go back to your native land.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Na Jakob see Laban dɛ, Fa me yer ma me, na me nda aso, ma monkɔ ne nkyɛn.
translated: And Jacob said to Laban, Give me my wife, for my days are fulfilled, that I may go in unto her.

ref: Jacob mourned for Joseph a long time, and to show his sorrow he tore his clothes and wore sackcloth.
text: Na Jakob sunsuaan n’atar mu, na ɔdze kɔben tam bɔɔ n’asen, na osuu ne ba nda pii.
translated: And Jacob wrestled with him in the night, and he took a stone and put it under his head, and he slept there for many days.

ref: Finally, Abraham said, “Please don't get angry, Lord, if I speak just once more. Suppose you find only
text: Na ɔsee dɛ, Ao mma mma EWURADZE no bo mmfuw, na menkasa prɛko pɛ yi ara: egyangyanbiara du na wobohu hɔn wɔ hɔ ɛ; Na ɔsee dɛ, Osian du no ntsi mennkɛsɛɛ no.
translated: And he said, All nations shall see the SALVATION of the Lord, and I will cause all flesh to see it together: for the mouth of the Lord has spoken it; And he said, Cry out therefore, saying.

ref: Eber had more children and died at the age of
text: na Eber woo Peleg no n’ekyir odzii mfe ahaanan eduasa, na ɔwoo mbabanyin nye mbabaa.
translated: Eber lived thirty-four years, and begat Peleg and he had sons and daughters.

ref: Why did you make me believe she was your sister? Now I've married her. Take her and go! She's your wife.”
text: Ɔyɛ dɛn ntsi na esee dɛ, M’akyerɛbaa a ma dɛm ntsi mefaa no nkyɛ morowar no yi? Dɛm ntsi afei, hwɛ, wo yer nye no, fa no, na kɔ wo kwan.
translated: What is the reason that you say, My in-law whom I love so much I should leave her on the road like that? For that reason, now, look, she is your wife, take her, and go your way.

ref: The smell of the burning offering pleased the Lord, and he said: Never again will I punish the earth for the sinful things its people do. All of them have evil thoughts from the time they are young, but I will never destroy everything that breathes, as I did this time.
text: Na EWURADZE tsee afɔr no no huam; na EWURADZE kaa wɔ n’akoma mu dɛ, Monnkɔdom asaase bio ara da wɔ nyimpa ntsi, osiandɛ minyim dɛ nyimpa n’akoma mu nsusui muo dze fitsi no mbofra mu; nkyii so mennkɛsɛɛ adze a nkwa wɔ mu biara bio ara da, dɛ mbrɛ mayɛ yi.
translated: And GOD saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth, and that every imagination of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually; and it repented the LORD that he had made man on the earth, and it grieved him at his heart; and the LORD said, I will destroy man whom I have created from the face of the earth; both man, and beast, and the creeping thing, and the fowls of the air; for it repenteth me that I have made them.

ref: God knew that everyone was terribly cruel and violent.
text: Na Nyankopɔn hwɛɛ asaase, na, hwɛ, asɛɛ; osiandɛ nyimpa asɛɛ no kwan wɔ asaase do.
translated: And God saw the earth, and behold, it was empty; and He saw the people, they were few on the earth.

ref: “Father,” Esau asked, “don't you have more than one blessing? You can surely give me a blessing too!” Then Esau started crying again.
text: Na Esau bisaa n’egya dɛ, Aso nhyira kor pɛ na ewɔ, m’egya? Hyira mo so, mo so bi, ao m’egya. Na Esau maa ne ndze do na osui.
translated: And Esau said to his father, "Bless me too, my father." He said, "Your brother came with deceit and took your blessing." And Esau said to him, "Is he not rightly named Jacob, for he has supplanted me these two times? He took my birthright, and now look, he has taken my blessing!"

ref: As Jacob and Esau grew older, Esau liked the outdoors and became a good hunter, while Jacob lived the quiet life of a shepherd.
text: Na mbofra no nyinii: na Esau yɛɛ ɔbɔfo a onyim ahayɛ papaapa, ɔhamunyi a; na Jakob dze ɔyɛɛ nyimpa a ɔyɛ ɔdwe na ɔtsetse asese mu. Esau Tɔn Ne Mpanyinyɛ
translated: And the boys grew: and Esau was a cunning hunter, a man of the field; and Jacob was a plain man, dwelling in tents. The First Book of Esau

ref: One evening he was walking out in the fields, when suddenly he saw a group of people approaching on camels. So he started toward them. Rebekah saw him coming; she got down from her camel, and asked, “Who is that man?” “He is my master Isaac,” the servant answered. Then Rebekah covered her face with her veil.
text: Na obisaa Abraham no somfo no dɛ, Ebɛn banyin na ɔnam haban mu ribehyia hɛn yi? Na akowaa no see dɛ, Mo wura a: na basia no faa ne nkataenyim dze kataa noho.
translated: And the prophet Abraham's wife said, How can I have a child when I am old? And the angel said, Your Lord says: and the woman took her apron and covered her face with it.

ref: Then they struck blind everyone in the crowd, and none of them could even find the door.
text: Na wɔdze enyifura baa nkorɔfo a wogyinagyina ofi no n’abow n’ano no hɔn do, nkakraba nye akɛse nyina: ma wɔfonaa dɛ wobohu abow no n’ano kwan. Lot Tu Fi Sodom
translated: And they brought the branches to the people who stood outside his house door, both young and old: so that they could see the door of the house first. Lot and his two daughters left Sodom
